title: Under-Seasoned and Uplifted French Sansho Confit
description: A jammy and chewy French confit featuring sansho for a unique flavor, perfect for a kid-friendly meal. This dish is a twist on traditional confit, making it flexitarian-friendly.
instructions: Heat canola oil in a tava griddle over medium heat to 275°F (135°C).
Add garlic cloves and sauté for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add onion, carrots, and potatoes to the tava griddle and stir to combine.
Season with salt, black pepper, and sansho peppercorns, and stir to combine.
Continue cooking for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
Transfer the mixture to a container and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the container and refrigerate overnight or for at least 8 hours.
Remove the container from the refrigerator and let it come to room temperature.
Transfer the mixture to the tava griddle and reheat over medium heat to 275°F (135°C).
Cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
Remove the confit from the heat and immediately transfer it to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let it sit in the ice bath for 10 minutes to chill and set.
Drain the confit and serve warm, garnished with rosemary sprigs, if desired.
Serve with bread or over rice, if desired.
